messages.content: hi can you personalise this text
Hi! How can I assist you today with personalizing this text?
add information about the school that id like to work for and why
Of course! Could you please provide me with some details about the school you would like to work for, as well as the reasons why it interests you?

At Ongar Place School we promote and teach a set of values to the children that will support them in their spiritual, moral, social and cultural development.  am very excited to apply for this position. I would like to be a part of the local community and feel that working in a local school to where I will be living gives back to the society and community. I am an enthusiastic, adaptable, and committed Primary Teacher with over 20 years’ experience. I have worked in schools both in and out of the UK in varying year groups and roles. I understand the need to be able to communicate effectively. My most recent role has been working at New Monument Primary school in Woking as a nursery teacher for children aged 2-3.Furthermore, when working with SEND children I understand that I need to send out information that is clear to form effective responses. I am multilingual so will use this skill to help families that may struggle in communicating in English alongside Google translate. I can understand the sensitivity involved in Send meetings with parents and other multidisciplinary teams. In the same vein I have excellent communication skills, both written and oral, which I have used to write up reports and consultations.
I am proud to say that I have also worked with younger and older children in various roles and settings over the last two years as a supply teacher  This experience has allowed me to showcase my versatility and adaptability in working with different age groups.

I am exceptionally thrilled to apply for a position at Ongar Place School. The values promoted and taught at the school, which support the spiritual, moral, social, and cultural development of children, resonate deeply with my own educational philosophy. Emphasizing these values sets a strong foundation for the holistic growth of students.

One aspect that particularly draws me to this school is the opportunity to contribute to the local community. I believe that working in a school closely aligned with my place of residence allows me to give back to society in a meaningful way. Being actively involved in the community and shaping the education of young minds creates a strong sense of fulfillment and purpose for me.

With over 20 years of experience as a Primary Teacher, both within the UK and internationally, I bring a wealth of knowledge and adaptability to the table. My most recent role as a nursery teacher at New Monument Primary School in Woking provided me with valuable experience in working with young children aged 2-3. This role enhanced my communication skills, enabling me to effectively convey information to parents and other multidisciplinary teams, particularly when supporting children with special educational needs. Additionally, being multilingual allows me to assist families who may struggle with English communication, utilizing both my language skills and tools like Google Translate.

During the past two years, I have also had the privilege of working with students of various ages as a supply teacher, showcasing my versatility and adaptability in different educational settings. This diverse experience has honed my abilities to cater to the unique needs of students across different year groups.

Overall, I am genuinely excited about the prospect of contributing to the growth and development of students at Ongar Place School. The alignment of values, the opportunity to serve the local community, and my extensive experience make me confident that I would be a valuable addition to the school's team.
hi
Hello! How can I assist you today?
can you change this text so it shorter but retains all the important information

I am excited to apply for a position at Ongar Place School, where values promoting students' holistic development are emphasized. Working in a local school allows me to give back to the community, aligning with my passion for local involvement. With 20+ years of experience, including international roles, I bring adaptability and effective communication skills. Being multilingual, I can assist non-English speaking families. My versatile background as a supply teacher has allowed me to work with diverse age groups. I am confident in my ability to contribute to the growth of students at Ongar Place School.

In terms of my credentials, I hold Qualified Teacher Status and have undergone training linked to KS1 or KS2. I also possess Enhanced DBS clearance, highlighting my commitment to ensuring a safe and effective learning environment for my students.

Overall, the combination of my qualifications, experience, and dedication to education makes me a strong candidate in the field of Early Years and KS1 teaching. I am excited to continue pursuing my passion for teaching and making a positive impact on young learners.
I have a strong background in Early Years education and a variety of experiences working with children of different ages. My qualifications, including my degree in Early Years, PGCE specialized in EYFS, and Montessori teaching diploma, demonstrate my expertise in this field. Additionally, my four years of experience as a year one teacher in a mainstream setting in the Kingston borough have provided me with practical knowledge and skills in excellent classroom management and practice.

Teaching Experience

I hold Qualified Teacher Status with specific training in KS1 and KS2. I also have Enhanced DBS clearance, ensuring a safe learning environment. With a strong background in Early Years education and diverse teaching experiences, including four years as a Year One teacher in the Kingston borough, my qualifications and practical knowledge contribute to effective classroom management and practice. I am dedicated to making a positive impact on young learners.
hi can you shorten this text but retain all the import points

I have Qualified Teacher Status with training in KS1 and KS2. Possessing Enhanced DBS clearance, I prioritize a safe learning environment. With experience as a Year One teacher in the Kingston borough, I excel in classroom management and practice. My background in Early Years education and various qualifications support my dedication to making a positive impact on young learners.

Teaching Experience
I have a strong background in teaching within the EYFS stemming from my early teaching career as a nursery nurse. My qualifications also reflect this. I am also aware that the EYFS curriculum has changed over the past few years and I have had in-house training on this. Currently I am working in a nursery attached to a mainstream school and have I PPA. every week with the nursery teacher from Broadmere Primary which is part of the academy . I have had long PPA Cover supply positions in two schools in very diverse setting within Yorkshire. This has given me experience in teaching lessons from nursery to year six. I have taught a wide variety of subjects both core and foundation. One of the schools, is a larger than the average-sized primary school. The proportion of disadvantaged, Eal pupils , and those who receive support for their SEND is higher than the national average. I managed to successfully teach these children and have been selected to return and teach there on several occasions due to my performance management in the setting. 
Professional skills and capabilities knowledge and understanding .
I have a passion for teaching and the ability to inspire and enthuse pupils and colleagues. I employ a growth mindset approach and appreciate the efforts of resilience that each child puts into their day. This is recognised through reward systems such as certificates, class Dojo, stickers or moving up the reward ladder. I am capable of planning rich learning experiences and drawing children into them through hooks and curiosity. To assess children, I use a variety of tools relevant to each setting. I use assessment for learning techniques because as a supply teacher I do not have any knowledge of the children I am teaching and where they are in their learning journey. I employ a variety of Afl techniques such as peer assessment, open ended tasks and activities. I also use round robin, Think-Pair-Share. Exit/Admit Tickets, talk partners, thumbs up thumbs down, and silent gallery. More recently I have used Cpoms and Arbor to assess monitor and safeguard children.

In terms of my teaching experience, I have a strong background in EYFS teaching, with previous roles as a nursery nurse and experience in teaching in diverse settings within Yorkshire. I have taught a wide range of subjects across nursery to year six and have successfully taught children from disadvantaged backgrounds, those with English as an additional language, and students with special educational needs. I am passionate about instilling a growth mindset in my students and employ various reward systems to recognize their efforts and resilience. I am skilled in planning engaging learning experiences and utilize a variety of assessment techniques, such as peer assessment and open-ended tasks, to ensure effective teaching and learning. Additionally, I am familiar with assessment tools like Cpoms and Arbor for monitoring and safeguarding children.
please shorten this text but retain the important inforamtion

I have a strong background in EYFS teaching and experience in diverse settings within Yorkshire. I have successfully taught children from diverse backgrounds and academic abilities. I am passionate about instilling a growth mindset in my students and utilize various assessment techniques to ensure effective teaching and learning.

I have set personalised challenge cards and targets for children, and a whole class target and understand the need for individual timetables and reward systems. As part of a whole school initiative, I took part in Case studies on how to support PPG children. I have used cross curricular planning and ensure that Foundation subjects are covered creatively and broadly. In the past I have used whole school data on pupil asset to help plan and move children on and have identified the steps of progress of children. These have been discussed with SLT in pupil information monitoring meetings. In these meetings I have proven that I have the ability in supporting children to expected or better than expected rates of progress and high achievement.
I have an excellent working knowledge of the KS1/KS2 curriculum and the expectation of standards as I have taught across the different key stages during supply cover. Furthermore, as RE lead I drew out progression maps for the subject from nursery to year 6. I led the development of a subject across the school and implemented the new RE syllabus which changes every five years.
I have been part of key stage one moderation meetings to assess judgements. Part of my role has entailed attending moderation meetings across the borough. More recently my role as intervention tutor looked at five schools within an academy that needed support and consistency in addressing the gaps in children’s learning. I therefore met with all the heads and planned with teachers the interventions. This is a vital component of effective planning and assessment. Observations carried out on my teaching show evidence of good and outstanding practice. My class also scored highly on their phonic screening tests. As part of all planning, I differentiate three ways. I teach at greater depth so that all learners are given the best scaffolding and modelling. To support learning, I present the learning objective. All children are given the opportunity to talk about the learning. There is an element of reading for the children. The learning is made accessible for all children depending on the type of learner they are and their needs e.g., visual and concrete resources. All lessons are ended with a plenary and children self-assess what they have learnt.

I have experience in setting personalized challenge cards and targets for students, as well as implementing whole class targets and individual timetables with appropriate reward systems. I actively participate in supporting PPG children through case studies and cross-curricular planning to ensure creative and comprehensive coverage of foundation subjects. I possess a strong working knowledge of the KS1/KS2 curriculum and have led the development and implementation of the RE syllabus. Additionally, I have actively engaged in moderation meetings and intervention planning to address learning gaps and ensure effective teaching and assessment. Observations of my teaching have shown evidence of good and outstanding practice, and my students have achieved high scores in phonic screening tests. I prioritize differentiation and teaching at greater depth to cater to the individual needs of my students, using visual and concrete resources to enhance learning. All lessons conclude with a self-assessment plenary to consolidate learning.

can motivate and manage pupils effectively by making the learning exciting and engaging. Very often I will use now and next cards. I use visual timetables and the zones of regulation to help children manage their feelings and emotions to learn effectively.
I have strong ICT competence and the ability to use ICT to enhance learning. To deliver lessons I have used the interactive white board (Promethium) and Smart notebook. I am familiar with the Teach Computing for primary school syllabus. Google Classroom has been used as a medium to send and celebrate learning and sometimes hold assemblies. I have employed Class Dojo to deliver communication with parents (EAL) and also set homework. Handwriting programmes Letterjoin and Penpals have been utilised alongside fine motor activities. Power Maths , White Rose and the Maths Mastery have been used alongside concert materials. For ICT I have used Beebots ,MacBooks and iPads with the children. I am confident in using desktops and laptops MacBooks and Apple Macs. More recently I have been using Tapestry , Kapow ,PHSE jigsaw and master the curriculum for maths.
I am highly motivated and managed to complete my degree as mature students whilst working and raising a family. I am an effective team player in any situation be it EYFS or the IR team.
Commitment and personal qualities 
I have a strong commitment to the safeguarding and well-being of all pupils. I ensure that I communicate and understand the needs of children ranging from their allergies to whom they are going home with. I keep up to date with The guidance outlined in the DfE’s (KCSIE.) I believe that there needs to be a strong relationship with all families. Inviting them in for celebrations assemblies, events, workshops and getting them engaged. I have high expectations and standards for all children and am committed in challenging them with a growth mindset approach. I have always been part of the wider school life and have been actively involved in PTA events. I have been involved in staff activities eg end of term lunches and have helped in afternoon clubs.
Interests
Details of any relevant interests or activities. I am creative and have developed a new interest in Islamic art and calligraphy. I have taken my passion and knowledge of perfumes and have developed a small business where I make and sell car perfumed air fresheners. I enjoy nature and have enjoyed forest schooling activities. Very often I will go for long walks in Surrey Hills

I effectively motivate and manage pupils by creating exciting and engaging learning experiences. I utilize visual aids such as now and next cards, visual timetables, and the zones of regulation to help children manage their emotions and facilitate effective learning. With strong ICT competence, I use interactive whiteboards and Smart notebook software for instructional delivery. I am familiar with Teach Computing for primary school and utilize platforms like Google Classroom for communication and to celebrate learning. I am proficient in various educational programs, including Letterjoin and Penpals for handwriting, Power Maths and White Rose for mathematics, and Tapestry and Kapow for online learning. Additionally, I am committed to the safeguarding and well-being of all pupils, maintaining open communication with families and ensuring high expectations for student growth. Outside of education, I maintain interests in Islamic art and calligraphy, perfume-making as a small business venture, and nature-related activities such as forest schooling and long walks in Surrey Hills.
